items.title: Security Updates Available for Adobe Commerce | APSB26-73
items.summary: This update resolves critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source. Successful exploitation could lead to arbitrary code execution, privilege escalation, and security feature bypass.

items.title: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type (Privilege Escalation)
items.summary: A critical file upload vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source that could lead to privilege escalation. This issue has a CVSS score of 9.6.

items.title: Improper Encoding or Escaping of Output (Arbitrary Code Execution)
items.summary: A critical vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source related to improper encoding or escaping of output, potentially leading to arbitrary code execution. This issue has a CVSS score of 9.1.

items.title: Cross-site Scripting (Stored XSS) (Privilege Escalation)
items.summary: A critical stored XSS vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source that could lead to privilege escalation. This issue has a CVSS score of 8.7.

items.title: Incorrect Authorization (Security Feature Bypass)
items.summary: A critical incorrect authorization vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source that could lead to security feature bypass. This issue has a CVSS score of 8.6.

items.title: Incorrect Authorization (Security Feature Bypass)
items.summary: A critical incorrect authorization vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source that could lead to security feature bypass. This issue has a CVSS score of 8.2.

items.title: Cross-site Scripting (Stored XSS) (Privilege Escalation) in B2B
items.summary: A critical stored XSS vulnerability affecting Adobe Commerce B2B, potentially leading to privilege escalation. This issue has a CVSS score of 8.1.

items.title: Incorrect Authorization (Security Feature Bypass)
items.summary: A critical incorrect authorization vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source that could lead to security feature bypass. This issue has a CVSS score of 7.6.

items.title: Improper Input Validation (Privilege Escalation)
items.summary: A critical improper input validation vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source that could lead to privilege escalation. This issue has a CVSS score of 7.2.

items.title: Critical Security Update: Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source (APSB26-05)
items.summary: This critical security update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source addresses multiple vulnerabilities. Successful exploitation could lead to security feature bypass, application denial-of-service, privilege escalation, arbitrary code execution, and arbitrary file system read.

items.title: PolyShell: unrestricted file upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical flaw in Magento's REST API that allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files to any store. This vulnerability, named PolyShell, uses a polyglot to bypass validation and can lead to remote code execution or account takeover.
